en.name: HUMAN SKIN
en.info: Inspect the layers of the skin in AR and tap the numbered labels to understand each protective and sensory structure.
en.hint: Use rotate, scale and labels while the skin model is active.

parts.label: Epidermis
parts.title: EPIDERMIS
parts.info: The epidermis is the thin outer layer that protects the body from the environment.
parts.hint: This surface layer forms the skin's first protective barrier.

parts.label: Dermis
parts.title: DERMIS
parts.info: The dermis supports the epidermis and contains vessels, nerves, follicles and glands.
parts.hint: This middle layer gives the skin strength and sensation.

parts.label: Hair Follicle
parts.title: HAIR FOLLICLE
parts.info: The hair follicle anchors a hair strand and supports its growth beneath the surface.
parts.hint: Look for the tube-like structure surrounding the hair root.

parts.label: Sebaceous Gland
parts.title: SEBACEOUS GLAND
parts.info: The sebaceous gland releases oil that helps keep the skin and hair from drying out.
parts.hint: This gland connects closely to the hair follicle.

parts.label: Sweat Gland
parts.title: SWEAT GLAND
parts.info: The sweat gland produces sweat to help regulate body temperature.
parts.hint: Its coiled shape sits deeper in the skin layers.

parts.label: Hypodermis
parts.title: HYPODERMIS
parts.info: The hypodermis is the deeper fatty layer that cushions the body and helps store energy.
parts.hint: This bottom layer helps anchor the skin to the tissues below.
